text.KJV: 1:1 Loue righteousnesse, yee that be iudges of the earth: thinke of the Lord with a good (heart) and in simplicitie of heart seeke him.
text.NABRE: 1:1 I. The Reward of Righteousness Chapter 1 Exhortation to Righteousness, the Key to Life Love righteousness, you who judge the earth; think of the Lord in goodness, and seek him in integrity of heart;
text.NRSV: Love righteousness, you rulers of the earth, think of the Lord in goodness and seek him with sincerity of heart;
text.DRB: Love justice, you that judge the earth. Think of our Lord in goodness, and in simplicity of heart seek him:

text.KJV: 1:2 For hee will bee found of them that tempt him not: and sheweth himselfe vnto such as doe not distrust him.
text.NABRE: 1:2 Because he is found by those who do not test him, and manifests himself to those who do not disbelieve him.
text.NRSV: because he is found by those who do not put him to the test, and manifests himself to those who do not distrust him.
text.DRB: because he is found of them that tempt him not: and he appeareth to them that have faith in him.

text.KJV: 1:3 For froward thoughts separate from God: and his power when it is tryed, reprooueth the vnwise.
text.NABRE: 1:3 For perverse counsels separate people from God, and his power, put to the proof, rebukes the foolhardy;
text.NRSV: For perverse thoughts separate people from God, and when his power is tested, it exposes the foolish;
text.DRB: For perverse cogitations separate from God: and proved power chasteneth the unwise:

text.KJV: 1:4 For into a malitious soule wisedome shall not enter: nor dwell in the body that is subiect vnto sinne.
text.NABRE: 1:4 Because into a soul that plots evil wisdom does not enter, nor does she dwell in a body under debt of sin.
text.NRSV: because wisdom will not enter a deceitful soul, or dwell in a body enslaved to sin.
text.DRB: because wisdom will not enter into a malicious soul, nor dwell in a body subject to sins.

text.KJV: 1:5 For the holy spirit of discipline will flie deceit, & remoue from thoughts that are without vnderstanding: and will not abide when vnrighteousnesse commeth in.
text.NABRE: 1:5 For the holy spirit of discipline flees deceit and withdraws from senseless counsels and is rebuked when unrighteousness occurs.
text.NRSV: For a holy and disciplined spirit will flee from deceit, and will leave foolish thoughts behind, and will be ashamed at the approach of unrighteousness.
text.DRB: For the Holy Ghost of discipline will fly from him that feigneth, and will withdraw himself from the cogitations that are without understanding, and he shall be chastened of iniquity ensuing.

text.KJV: 1:6 For wisedome is a louing spirit: and will not acquite a blasphemour of his words: for God is witnesse of his reines, and a true beholder of his heart, and a hearer of his tongue.
text.NABRE: 1:6 For wisdom is a kindly spirit, yet she does not acquit blasphemous lips; Because God is the witness of the inmost self and the sure observer of the heart and the listener to the tongue.
text.NRSV: For wisdom is a kindly spirit, but will not free blasphemers from the guilt of their words; because God is witness of their inmost feelings, and a true observer of their hearts, and a hearer of their tongues.
text.DRB: For the spirit of wisdom is gentle, and will not deliver the curser from his lips: because God is witness of his reins, and he is a true searcher of his heart, and an hearer of his tongue.

text.KJV: 1:7 For the spirit of the Lord filleth the world: and that which containeth all things hath knowledge of the voice.
text.NABRE: 1:7 For the spirit of the Lord fills the world, is all-embracing, and knows whatever is said.
text.NRSV: Because the spirit of the Lord has filled the world, and that which holds all things together knows what is said,
text.DRB: Because the Spirit of our Lord hath replenished the whole world: and that which containeth all things, hath the knowledge of voice.

text.KJV: 1:8 Therefore he that speaketh vnrighteous things, cannot be hid: neither shal vengeance, when it punisheth. passe by him.
text.NABRE: 1:8 Therefore those who utter wicked things will not go unnoticed, nor will chastising condemnation pass them by.
text.NRSV: therefore those who utter unrighteous things will not escape notice, and justice, when it punishes, will not pass them by.
text.DRB: For this cause he that speaketh unjust things, cannot be hid, neither shall the chastising judgement pass him.
For in the cogitations of the impious there shall be examination: and the hearing of his works shall come to God, to the chastising of his iniquities.
text.KJV: 1:9 For inquisition shall be made into the counsels of the vngodly: and the sound of his words, shall come vnto the Lord, for the manifestation of his wicked deedes.

text.DRB: Because the ear of jealousy heareth all things, and the tumult of murmurings shall not be hid.
text.KJV: 1:10 For the eare of iealousie heareth al things: and the noise of murmurings is not hid.

text.DRB: Keep yourselves therefore from murmuring, which profiteth nothing, and refrain your tongue from detraction, because an obscure speech shall not pass in vain: and the mouth that lieth, killeth the soul.
text.KJV: 1:11 Therefore beware of murmuring, which is vnprofitable, and refraine your tongue from backbiting: for there is no word so secret that shall goe for nought: and the mouth that belieth, slayeth the soule.
1:12 Seeke not death in the errour of your life: and pull not vpon your selues destruction, with the workes of your hands.
text.NABRE: 1:12 Do not court death by your erring way of life, nor draw to yourselves destruction by the works of your hands.
text.NRSV: Do not invite death by the error of your life, or bring on destruction by the works of your hands;
text.DRB: Zeal not death in the error of your life, neither procure ye perdition by the works of your hands.

text.KJV: 1:13 For God made not death: neither hath he pleasure in the destruction of the liuing.
text.NABRE: 1:13 Because God did not make death, nor does he rejoice in the destruction of the living.
text.NRSV: because God did not make death, and he does not delight in the death of the living.
text.DRB: Because God made not death, neither doth he rejoice in the perdition of the living.

text.KJV: 1:14 For he created all things, that they might haue their being: and the generations of the world were healthfull: and there is no poyson of destruction in them: nor the kingdome of death vpon the earth.
text.NABRE: 1:14 For he fashioned all things that they might have being, and the creatures of the world are wholesome; There is not a destructive drug among them nor any domain of Hades on earth,
text.NRSV: For he created all things so that they might exist; the generative forces of the world are wholesome, and there is no destructive poison in them, and the dominion of Hades is not on earth.
text.DRB: For he created all things to be: and he made the nations of the earth to health: and there is no medicine of destruction in them, nor Kingdom of hell in the earth.

text.KJV: 1:15 For righteousnesse is immortall.
text.NABRE: 1:15 For righteousness is undying.
text.NRSV: For righteousness is immortal.
text.DRB: For justice is perpetual and immortal.

text.KJV: 1:16 But vngodly men with their workes, and words called it to them: for when they thought to haue it their friend, they consumed to nought, and made a couenant with it, because they are worthy to take part with it.
text.NABRE: 1:16 The Wicked Reject Immortality and Righteousness Alike It was the wicked who with hands and words invited death, considered it a friend, and pined for it, and made a covenant with it, Because they deserve to be allied with it.
text.NRSV: But the ungodly by their words and deeds summoned death; considering him a friend, they pined away and made a covenant with him, because they are fit to belong to his company.
text.DRB: But the impious with hands & words have provoked it: and esteeming it a friend, have fallen to decay, and have made covenants with it: because they are worthy to be of the part thereof.

text.KJV: 2:1 For the vngodly said, reasoning with themselues, but not aright: Our life is short and tedious, and in the death of a man there is no remedie: neither was there any man knowen to haue returned from the graue.
text.NABRE: 2:1 Chapter 2 For, not thinking rightly, they said among themselves: “Brief and troubled is our lifetime; there is no remedy for our dying, nor is anyone known to have come back from Hades.
text.NRSV: For they reasoned unsoundly, saying to themselves, “Short and sorrowful is our life, and there is no remedy when a life comes to its end, and no one has been known to return from Hades.”
text.DRB: For they have said thinking with themselves not well: Little, and with tediousness is the time of our life: and in the end of a man there is no recovery, and there is none known that hath returned from hell:

text.KJV: 2:2 For wee are borne at all aduenture: & we shalbe heereafter as though we had neuer bene: for the breath in our nostrils is as smoke, and a litle sparke in the mouing of our heart.
text.NABRE: 2:2 For by mere chance were we born, and hereafter we shall be as though we had not been; Because the breath in our nostrils is smoke, and reason a spark from the beating of our hearts,
text.NRSV: “For we were born by mere chance, and hereafter we shall be as though we had never been, for the breath in our nostrils is smoke, and reason is a spark kindled by the beating of our hearts;”
text.DRB: because of nothing were we born, and after this we shall be as if we had not been: because the breath is a smoke in our nostrils: & speech a spark to move our heart.

Which being extinguished, our body shall be ashes, and the spirit shall be poured abroad as soft air, and our life shall pass as the trace of a cloud, and shall be dissolved as a mist, which is driven away by the beams of the sun, and oppressed with the heat thereof:
text.KJV: 2:3 Which being extinguished, our body shall be turned into ashes, and our spirit shall vanish as the soft aire:

text.DRB: and our name in time shall be forgotten, and no man shall have remembrance of our works.
text.KJV: 2:4 And our name shalbe forgotten in time, and no man shall haue our works in remembrance, and our life shall passe away as the trace of a cloud: and shall be dispersed as a mist that is driuen away with the beames of the Sunne, and ouercome with the heat thereof.

text.DRB: For our time is the passing of a shadow, and there is no return of our end: because it is sealed, and no man returneth.
text.KJV: 2:5 For our time is a very shadow that passeth away: and after our end there is no returning: for it is fast sealed, so that no man commeth againe.
2:6 Come on therefore, let vs enioy the good things that are present: and let vs speedily vse the creatures like as in youth.
text.NABRE: 2:6 Come, therefore, let us enjoy the good things that are here, and make use of creation with youthful zest.
text.NRSV: “Come, therefore, let us enjoy the good things that exist, and make use of the creation to the full as in youth.”
text.DRB: Come therefore, and let us enjoy the good things that are, and let us quickly use the creature as in youth.
Let us fill ourselves with precious wine, and ointments: and let not the flower of the time pass us.
text.KJV: 2:7 Let vs fill our selues with costly wine, and ointments: and let no flower of the Spring passe by vs.

text.DRB: Let us crown ourselves with roses, before they wither: let there be no meadow, which our riot shall not pass through.
text.KJV: 2:8 Let vs crowne our selues with Rose buds, before they be withered.

text.DRB: Let none of us be exempted from our riotousness: every-where let us leave signs of joy: because this is our portion, and this our lot.
text.KJV: 2:9 Let none of vs goe without his part of our voluptuousnesse: let vs leaue tokens of our ioyfulnesse in euery place: for this is our portion, and our lot is this.

text.DRB: Let us oppress the poor just man, and not spare the widow, nor reverence the old man's grey head of long time.
text.KJV: 2:10 Let vs oppresse the poore righteous man, let vs not spare the widow, nor reuerence the ancient gray haires of the aged.

text.DRB: But let our strength be the law of justice: for that which is weak, is found unprofitable.
text.KJV: 2:11 Let our strength bee the Lawe of iustice: for that which is feeble is found to be nothing worth.

2:12 Therefore let vs lye in wait for the righteous: because he is not for our turne, and he is cleane contrary to our doings: he vpbraideth vs with our offending the Law, and obiecteth to our infamy the transgressings of our education.
text.NABRE: 2:12 Let us lie in wait for the righteous one, because he is annoying to us; he opposes our actions, Reproaches us for transgressions of the law and charges us with violations of our training.
text.NRSV: “Let us lie in wait for the righteous man, because he is inconvenient to us and opposes our actions; he reproaches us for sins against the law, and accuses us of sins against our training.”
text.DRB: Let us therefore circumvent the just, because he is unprofitable to us, and he is contrary to our works, and reproachfully objecteth unto us the sins of the law, and defameth in us the sins of our discipline.

text.KJV: 2:13 Hee professeth to haue the knowledge of God: and hee calleth himselfe the childe of the Lord.
text.NABRE: 2:13 He professes to have knowledge of God and styles himself a child of the Lord.
text.NRSV: “He professes to have knowledge of God, and calls himself a child of the Lord.”
text.DRB: He boasteth that he hath the knowledge of God, and nameth himself the son of God.
He is made unto us to the defaming of our cogitations.
text.KJV: 2:14 Hee was made to reprooue our thoughts.

text.DRB: He is grievous unto us even to behold, because his life is unlike to others, & his ways are changed.
text.KJV: 2:15 Hee is grieuous vnto vs euen to beholde: for his life is not like other mens, his waies are of another fashion.

text.DRB: We are esteemed of him as triflers, and he abstaineth from our ways as from uncleanness, and he preferreth the later ends of the just, and glorieth that he hath God for his father.
text.KJV: 2:16 We are esteemed of him as counterfeits: he abstaineth from our wayes as from filthinesse: he pronounceth the end of the iust to be blessed, and maketh his boast that God is his father.

text.DRB: Let us see therefore if his words be true, and let us prove what things shall come to him, and we shall know what shall be his later end.
text.KJV: 2:17 Let vs see if his wordes be true: and let vs proue what shall happen in the end of him.

text.DRB: For if he be the true son of God, he will defend him, & will deliver him from the hands of the adversaries.
text.KJV: 2:18 For if the iust man be the sonne of God, he will helpe him, and deliuer him from the hand of his enemies.

text.DRB: By contumely and torment let us examine him, that we may know his reverence, and prove his patience.
text.KJV: 2:19 Let vs examine him with despitefulnesse and torrture, that we may know his meekenesse, and prooue his patience.

text.DRB: To a most shameful death let us condemn him: for there shall be respect had unto him by his words.
text.KJV: 2:20 Let vs condemne him with a shamefull death: for by his owne saying, he shall be respected.
2:21 Such things they did imagine, and were deceiued: for their owne wickednesse hath blinded them.
text.NABRE: 2:21 These were their thoughts, but they erred; for their wickedness blinded them,
text.NRSV: Thus they reasoned, but they were led astray, for their wickedness blinded them,
text.DRB: These things have they thought, and have erred: for their malice hath blinded them.

text.KJV: 2:22 As for the mysteries of God, they knew them not: neither hoped they for the wages of righteousnesse: nor discerned a reward for blamelesse soules.
text.NABRE: 2:22 And they did not know the hidden counsels of God; neither did they count on a recompense for holiness nor discern the innocent souls’ reward.
text.NRSV: and they did not know the secret purposes of God, nor hoped for the wages of holiness, nor discerned the prize for blameless souls.
text.DRB: And they have not known the sacraments of God, nor hoped for the reward of justice, nor esteemed the honour of holy souls.

text.KJV: 2:23 For God created man to bee immortall, and made him to be an image of his owne eternitie.
text.NABRE: 2:23 For God formed us to be imperishable; the image of his own nature he made us.
text.NRSV: for God created us for incorruption, and made us in the image of his own eternity.
text.DRB: For God created man incorruptible, and to the image of his own likeness he made him.

text.KJV: 2:24 Neuerthelesse through enuie of the deuill came death into the world: and they that doe holde of his side doe finde it.
text.NABRE: 2:24 But by the envy of the devil, death entered the world, and they who are allied with him experience it.
text.NRSV: but through the devil’s envy death entered the world, and those who belong to his company experience it.
text.DRB: But by the envy of the devil, death entered into the world:
and they follow him that are of his part.

text.KJV: 3:1 But the soules of the righteous are in the hand of God, and there shall no torment touch them.
text.NABRE: 3:1 Chapter 3 The Hidden Counsels of God A. On Suffering The souls of the righteous are in the hand of God, and no torment shall touch them.
text.NRSV: But the souls of the righteous are in the hand of God, and no torment will ever touch them.
text.DRB: But the souls of the just are in the hand of God, and the torment of death shall not touch them.

text.KJV: 3:2 In the sight of the vnwise they seemed to die: and their departure is taken for misery,
text.NABRE: 3:2 They seemed, in the view of the foolish, to be dead; and their passing away was thought an affliction
text.NRSV: In the eyes of the foolish they seemed to have died, and their departure was thought to be a disaster,
text.DRB: They seemed in the eyes of the unwise to die: and their decease was counted affliction:

text.KJV: 3:3 And their going from vs to be vtter destruction: but they are in peace.
text.NABRE: 3:3 and their going forth from us, utter destruction. But they are in peace.
text.NRSV: and their going from us to be their destruction; but they are at peace.
text.DRB: and that which with us is the way, is destruction: but they are in peace.

text.KJV: 3:4 For though they bee punished in the sight of men: yet is their hope full of immortalitie.
text.NABRE: 3:4 For if to others, indeed, they seem punished, yet is their hope full of immortality;
text.NRSV: For though in the sight of others they were punished, their hope is full of immortality.
text.DRB: And though before men they suffered torments, their hope is full of immortality.

text.KJV: 3:5 And hauing bene a little chastised, they shalbe greatly rewarded: for God proued them, and found them worthy for himselfe.
text.NABRE: 3:5 Chastised a little, they shall be greatly blessed, because God tried them and found them worthy of himself.
text.NRSV: Having been disciplined a little, they will receive great good, because God tested them and found them worthy of himself.
text.DRB: Vexed in few things, in many they shall be well disposed of: because God hath tempted them, and hath found them worthy of himself.

text.KJV: 3:6 As gold in the furnace hath hee tried them, and receiued them as a burnt offering.
text.NABRE: 3:6 As gold in the furnace, he proved them, and as sacrificial offerings he took them to himself.
text.NRSV: Like gold in the furnace he tried them, and like a sacrificial burnt offering he accepted them.
text.DRB: As gold in the furnace he hath proved them, and as an host of holocaust he hath received them, & in time there shall be respect of them.

text.KJV: 3:7 And in the time of their visitation, they shall shine and runne to and fro, like sparkes among the stubble.
text.NABRE: 3:7 In the time of their judgment they shall shine and dart about as sparks through stubble;
text.NRSV: In the time of their visitation they will shine forth, and will run like sparks through the stubble.
text.DRB: The just shall shine, and as sparks in a place of reeds they shall run abroad.

text.KJV: 3:8 They shall iudge the nations, and haue dominion ouer the people, and their Lord shall raigne for euer.
text.NABRE: 3:8 They shall judge nations and rule over peoples, and the Lord shall be their King forever.
text.NRSV: They will govern nations and rule over peoples, and the Lord will reign over them forever.
text.DRB: They shall judge nations, & have dominion over peoples, and their Lord shall reign for ever.

text.KJV: 3:9 They that put their trust in him, shall vnderstand the trueth: and such as be faithfull in loue, shall abide with him: for grace & mercy is to his saints, and he hath care for his elect.
text.NABRE: 3:9 Those who trust in him shall understand truth, and the faithful shall abide with him in love: Because grace and mercy are with his holy ones, and his care is with the elect.
text.NRSV: Those who trust in him will understand truth, and the faithful will abide with him in love, because grace and mercy are upon his holy ones, and he watches over his elect.
text.DRB: They that trust in him, shall understand truth: & the faithful in love shall rest in him: because rest and peace is to his elect.
But the impious according to the things which they have thought, shall have correption: which have neglected the just, & have revolted from our Lord.
text.KJV: 3:10 But the vngodly shalbe punished according to their owne imaginations, which haue neglected the righteous, and forsaken the Lord.

text.DRB: For he that rejecteth wisdom and discipline, is unhappy: and their hope is vain, and labours without fruit, & their works unprofitable.
text.KJV: 3:11 For who so despiseth wisedome, and nurture, he is miserable, and their hope is vaine, their labours vnfruitfull, and their works vnprofitable.

text.DRB: Their wives are senseless, and their children most wicked.
text.KJV: 3:12 Their wiues are foolish, and their children wicked.

text.DRB: Cursed is their creature: because happy is the barren woman: and the undefiled, which hath not known bed in sin, she shall have fruit in visitation of holy souls:
text.KJV: 3:13 Their of-spring is cursed: wherefore blessed is the barren that is vndefiled, which hath not knowen the sinfull bed: she shall haue fruit in the visitation of soules.

text.DRB: and the eunuch that hath not wrought iniquity with his hands, nor thought most wicked things against God: for the chosen gift of faith shall be given to him, and a most acceptable lot in the temple of God.
text.KJV: 3:14 And blessed is the Eunuch which with his hands hath wrought no iniquitie: nor imagined wicked things against God: for vnto him shall be giuen the speciall gift of faith, and an inheritance in the Temple of the Lord more acceptable to his minde.

text.DRB: For of good labour there is glorious fruit, and the root of wisdom which falleth not.
text.KJV: 3:15 For glorious is the fruit of good labours: and the root of wisedom shall neuer fall away.

text.DRB: But the children of adulterers shall be in consummation, and the seed of the unlawful bed shall be destroyed.
text.KJV: 3:16 As for the children of adulterers, they shall not come to their perfection, and the seed of an vnrighteous bed shal be rooted out.

text.DRB: And if certes they be of long life, they shall be reputed for nothing, & their last old-age shall be without honour.
text.KJV: 3:17 For though they liue long, yet shall they bee nothing regarded: and their last age shall be without honour.

text.DRB: And if they die quickly they shall have no hope, nor speech of comfort in the day of acknowledging.
text.KJV: 3:18 Or if they die quickly, they haue no hope, neither comfort in the day of triall.

text.DRB: For of a wicked nation the ends are cruel.
text.KJV: 3:19 For horrible is the end of the vnrighteous generation.
4:1 Better it is to haue no children, and to haue vertue: for the memoriall thereof is immortal: because it is knowen with God and with men.
text.NABRE: 4:1 Chapter 4 Better is childlessness with virtue; for immortal is the memory of virtue, acknowledged both by God and human beings.
text.NRSV: Better is childlessness with virtue, for in the memory of virtue is immortality, because it is known both by God and by mortals.
text.DRB: Ohow beautiful is the chaste generation with glory! For the memory thereof is immortal: because it is known both with God and with men.
When it is present, they imitate it, and they desire it when it hath withdrawn it-self, and it triumpheth crowned for ever, winning the reward of undefiled conflicts.
text.KJV: 4:2 When it is present, men take example at it, and when it is gone they desire it: it weareth a crown, and triumpheth for euer, hauing gotten the victorie, striuing for vndefiled rewards.

text.DRB: But the multitude of the impious, that hath many children, shall not be profitable, and bastard plants shall not take deep root, nor lay sure foundation.
text.KJV: 4:3 But the multiplying brood of the vngodly shall not thriue, nor take deepe rooting from bastard slips, nor lay any fast foundation.

text.DRB: And if in the boughs for a time they shall spring being weakly set, they shall be moved of the wind, and by the vehemency of the winds they shall be rooted out.
text.KJV: 4:4 For though they flourish in branches for a time: yet standing not fast, they shall be shaken with the winde: and through the force of windes they shall be rooted out.

text.DRB: For the unperfect boughs shall be broken, and their fruits shall be unprofitable, and sour to eat, and meet for nothing.
text.KJV: 4:5 The vnperfect branches shall bee broken off, their fruit vnprofitable, not ripe to eate: yea meet for nothing.

text.DRB: For the children that be born of wicked sleeps, are witnesses of wickedness against the parents in their examination.
text.KJV: 4:6 For children begotten of vnlawfull beds, are witnesses of wickednes against their parents in their triall.
4:7 But though the righteous be preuented with death: yet shal he be in rest.
text.NABRE: 4:7 C. On Early Death But the righteous one, though he die early, shall be at rest.
text.NRSV: But the righteous, though they die early, will be at rest.
text.DRB: But the just if he be prevented with death, shall be in a place of refreshing.

text.KJV: 4:8 For honourable age is not that which standeth in length of time, nor that is measured by number of yeeres.
text.NABRE: 4:8 For the age that is honorable comes not with the passing of time, nor can it be measured in terms of years.
text.NRSV: For old age is not honored for length of time, or measured by number of years;
text.DRB: For venerable old age is not that of long time, nor accounted by the number of years: but the understanding of a man are grey hairs:

text.KJV: 4:9 But wisedome is the gray haire vnto men, & an vnspotted life is old age.
text.NABRE: 4:9 Rather, understanding passes for gray hair, and an unsullied life is the attainment of old age.
text.NRSV: but understanding is gray hair for anyone, and a blameless life is ripe old age.
text.DRB: and an immaculate life is old age.

text.KJV: 4:10 He pleased God, and was beloued of him: so that liuing amongst sinners, he was translated.
text.NABRE: 4:10 The one who pleased God was loved, living among sinners, was transported—
text.NRSV: There were some who pleased God and were loved by him, and while living among sinners were taken up.
text.DRB: Pleasing God he is made beloved, and living among sinners he was translated.

text.KJV: 4:11 Yea, speedily was he taken away, lest that wickednes should alter his vnderstanding, or deceit beguile his soule.
text.NABRE: 4:11 Snatched away, lest wickedness pervert his mind or deceit beguile his soul;
text.NRSV: They were caught up so that evil might not change their understanding or guile deceive their souls.
text.DRB: He was taken away lest malice should change his understanding, or lest any guile might deceive his soul.
For the bewitching of vanity obscureth good things, and the inconstancy of concupiscence perverteth the understanding that is without malice.
text.KJV: 4:12 For the bewitching of naughtines doth obscure things that are honest: and the wandring of concupiscence, doth vndermine the simple mind.

text.DRB: Being consummate in short space he fulfilled much time:
text.KJV: 4:13 He being made perfect in a short time, fulfilled a long time.

text.DRB: for his soul pleased God: for this cause he hastened to bring him out of the midst of iniquities: but the peoples that are seeing, and not understanding, nor putting such things in their hearts:
text.KJV: 4:14 For his soule pleased the Lord: therefore hasted he to take him away, from among the wicked.

text.DRB: that the grace of God, and mercy is toward his saints, and respect toward his elect.
text.KJV: 4:15 This the people saw, and vnderstood it not: neither laid they vp this in their mindes, That his grace and mercie is with his Saints, and that he hath respect vnto his chosen.

text.DRB: But the just dead condemneth the impious alive, and youth soon ended, the long life of the unjust.
text.KJV: 4:16 Thus the righteous that is dead, shall condemne the vngodly, which are liuing, and youth that is soone perfected, the many yeeres and old age of the vnrighteous.

text.DRB: For they shall see the end of the wise, and shall not understand what God hath thought of him, and why our Lord hath fenced him.
text.KJV: 4:17 For they shall see the end of the wise, & shall not vnderstand what God in his counsell hath decreed of him, and to what end the Lord hath set him in safetie.

text.DRB: For they shall see and shall contemn him: but our Lord shall laugh them to scorn,
text.KJV: 4:18 They shal see him and despise him, but God shall laugh them to scorne, and they shal hereafter be a vile carkeis, and a reproch among the dead for euermore.

text.DRB: and they shall fall after this without honour, and in contumely among the dead for ever: because he shall break them puffed up without voice, and shall remove them from the foundations, and they shall be made desolate unto the highest degree: and shall be mourning, and the memory of them shall perish.
text.KJV: 4:19 For he shall rend them, and cast them downe headlong, that they shalbe speechles: and he shal shake them from the foundation: and they shall bee vtterly laid waste, and be in sorow: and their memoriall shall perish.

text.DRB: They shall come fearful in cogitation of their sins, and their iniquities on the contrary shall convince them.
text.KJV: 4:20 And when they cast vp the accounts of their sinnes, they shall come with feare: and their owne iniquities shall conuince them to their face.
5:1 Then shal the righteous man stand in great boldnesse, before the face of such as haue afflicted him, and made no account of his labours.
text.NABRE: 5:1 Chapter 5 Then shall the righteous one with great assurance confront his oppressors who set at nought his labors.
text.NRSV: Then the righteous will stand with great confidence in the presence of those who have oppressed them and those who make light of their labors.
text.DRB: Then shall the just stand in great constancy against those that have afflicted them, and taken away their labours.

text.KJV: 5:2 When they see it, they shalbe troubled with terrible feare, & shall be amazed at the strangenesse of his saluation, so farre beyond all that they looked for.
text.NABRE: 5:2 Seeing this, the wicked shall be shaken with dreadful fear, and be amazed at the unexpected salvation.
text.NRSV: When the unrighteous see them, they will be shaken with dreadful fear, and they will be amazed at the unexpected salvation of the righteous.
text.DRB: They seeing shall be troubled with horrible fear, and shall marvel at the suddenness of unexpected salvation,
saying within themselves, repenting, and sighing for anguish of spirit: These are they, whom we had sometime in derision, and in a parable of reproach.
text.KJV: 5:3 And they repenting, and groning for anguish of spirit, shall say within themselues, This was he whom wee had sometimes in derision, and a prouerbe of reproch.
5:4 We fooles accounted his life madnes, and his end to be without honour.
text.NABRE: 5:4 fools that we were! His life we accounted madness, and death dishonored.
text.NRSV: “We fools thought their life a madness and their end to be without honor.”
text.DRB: We senseless esteemed their life madness, and their end without honour.

text.KJV: 5:5 How is hee numbred among the children of God, and his lot is among the Saints?
text.NABRE: 5:5 See how he is accounted among the heavenly beings; how his lot is with the holy ones!
text.NRSV: “How have they been numbered among the children of God? And their lot is among the saints.”
text.DRB: Behold how they are counted among the children of God, and their lot is among the saints.
We therefore have erred from the way of truth, and the light of justice hath not shined to us, and the sun of understanding rose not to us.
text.KJV: 5:6 Therefore haue wee erred from the way of trueth, and the light of righteousnesse hath not shined vnto vs, and the Sunne of righteousnesse rose not vpon vs.

text.DRB: We are wearied in the way of iniquity and perdition, and have walked hard ways, but the way of our Lord we have not known.
text.KJV: 5:7 We wearied ourselues in the way of wickednesse, and destruction: yea, we haue gone through deserts, where there lay no way: but as for the way of the Lord, we haue not knowen it.

text.DRB: What hath pride profited us? Or what commodity hath the vaunting of riches brought to us?
text.KJV: 5:8 What hath pride profited vs? or what good hath riches with our vaunting brought vs?

text.DRB: All those things are passed away as a shadow, and as a messenger running before,
text.KJV: 5:9 All those things are passed away like a shadow, and as a Poste that hasted by.

text.DRB: and as a ship, that passeth through the surging waters: whereof, when it is past, the trace cannot be found nor the path of that ship's keel in the waves:
text.KJV: 5:10 And as a ship that passeth ouer the waues of the water, which when it is gone by, the trace thereof cannot bee found: neither the path way of the keele in the waues.

text.DRB: or as a bird, that flyeth through in the air, of which there is no token can be found of her passage, but only a sound of the wings beating the light wind: and by vehemence of going cutting the air, moving the wings she is flown through, and afterward there is no sign found of her way:
text.KJV: 5:11 Or as when a bird hath flowen thorow the aire, there is no token of her way to be found, but the light aire being beaten with the stroke of her wings, and parted with the violent noise and motion of them, is passed thorow, and therin afterwards no signe where she went, is to be found.

text.DRB: or as when an arrow is shot forth to a set mark, the divided air is forthwith closed in it-self again, so that the passage thereof is not known:
text.KJV: 5:12 Or like as when an arrow is shot at a marke, it parteth the aire, which immediatly commeth together againe: so that a man cannot know where it went thorow:

text.DRB: so we also being born forthwith ceased to be: and of virtue certes have been able to shew no sign: but in our naughtiness we are consumed.
text.KJV: 5:13 Euen so we in like maner, assoone as we were borne, began to draw to our end, and had no signe of vertue to shew: but were consumed in our owne wickednesse.

text.DRB: Such things said they in hell, which sinned:
text.KJV: 5:14 For the hope of the vngodly is like dust that is blowen away with þ wind, like a thinne froth that is driuen away with þ storme: like as the smoke which is dispersed here and there with a tempest, and passeth away as the remembrance of a guest that tarieth but a day.
5:15 But þ righteous liue for euermore, their reward also is with the Lord, and the care of them is with the most High.
text.NABRE: 5:15 But the righteous live forever, and in the Lord is their recompense, and the thought of them is with the Most High.
text.NRSV: But the righteous live forever, and their reward is with the Lord; the Most High takes care of them.
text.DRB: because the hope of the impious is as dust, which is taken away with the wind: and as a thin froth, which is dispersed by the storm: and as smoke that is scattered abroad by the wind: and as the memory of a guest of one day that passeth.

text.KJV: 5:16 Therfore shall they receiue a glorious kingdome, & a beautiful crowne from the Lords hande: for with his right hand shall he couer them, and with his arme shall he protect them.
text.NABRE: 5:16 Therefore shall they receive the splendid crown, the beautiful diadem, from the hand of the Lord, For he will shelter them with his right hand, and protect them with his arm.
text.NRSV: Therefore they will receive a glorious crown and a beautiful diadem from the hand of the Lord, because with his right hand he will cover them, and with his arm he will shield them.
text.DRB: But the just shall live for ever, and their reward is with our Lord, and cogitation of them with the Highest.
Therefore shall they receive a Kingdom of honour, & a crown of beauty at the hand of our Lord: because with his right hand he will cover them, and within his holy arm he will defend them.
text.KJV: 5:17 He shall take to him his ielousie for cōplete armour, & make the creature his weapon for the reuenge of his enemies.

text.DRB: And his zeal will take armour, and he will arm the creature to the revenge of the enemies.
text.KJV: 5:18 He shal put on righteousnesse as a brestplate, and true iudgement in stead of an helmet.

text.DRB: He will put on justice for a breast-plate, & will take sincere judgement for an helmet:
text.KJV: 5:19 He shall take holinesse for an inuincible shield.

text.DRB: he will take equity for an invincible shield:
text.KJV: 5:20 His seuere wrath shall he sharpen for a sword, and the world shall fight with him against the vnwise.

text.DRB: and he will sharpen fierce wrath for a spear, & the round world shall fight with him against the senseless.
text.KJV: 5:21 Then shal the right-aiming thunder bolts goe abroad, and from the cloudes, as from a well-drawen bow, shall they flie to the marke.

text.DRB: The shots of lightnings shall go directly, & as it were from a bow of the clouds well bent they shall be cast forth, and shall light on a certain place.
text.KJV: 5:22 And hailestones full of wrath shal be cast as out of a stonebow, and the water of the Sea shall rage against them, & the floods shall cruelly drowne them.

text.DRB: And from rocked wrath shall thick hail-stones be cast, the water of the sea shall rage against them, and the rivers shall run together roughly.
text.KJV: 5:23 Yea a mightie wind shall stand vp against them, & like a storme shall blow them away: thus iniquity shal lay wast the whole earth, and ill dealing shall ouerthrow the thrones of the mightie.

text.DRB: Aspirit of power shall stand against them, and as a hurlwind shall divide them: and their iniquity shall bring all the land to a desert, and naughtiness shall overthrow the seats of the mighty.

text.KJV: 6:1 Heare therefore, O yee kings, and vnderstand, learne yee that be iudges of the ends of the earth.
text.NABRE: 6:1 Chapter 6 Exhortation to Seek Wisdom Hear, therefore, kings, and understand; learn, you magistrates of the earth’s expanse!
text.NRSV: Listen therefore, O kings, and understand; learn, O judges of the ends of the earth.
text.DRB: Wisdom is better than strength: and a wise man than a strong.
Hear therefore ye kings, & understand, learn ye judges of the ends of the earth.
text.KJV: 6:2 Giue eare you that rule the people, and glory in the multitude of nations.
6:3 For power is giuen you of the Lord, & soueraigntie from the Highest, who shall try your workes; and search out your counsels.
text.NABRE: 6:3 Because authority was given you by the Lord and sovereignty by the Most High, who shall probe your works and scrutinize your counsels!
text.NRSV: For your dominion was given you from the Lord, and your sovereignty from the Most High; he will search out your works and inquire into your plans.
text.DRB: Give ear ye that rule multitudes, and that please yourselves in multitudes of nations:
because the power is given you of our Lord, and strength by the Highest, who will examine your works, and search your cogitations:
text.KJV: 6:4 Because being Ministers of his kingdome, you haue not iudged aright, nor kept the law, nor walked after the counsell of God,

text.DRB: because when you were the ministers of his Kingdom, you judged not rightly, nor kept the law of justice, nor have walked according to the will of God.
text.KJV: 6:5 Horribly and speedily shall he come vpon you: for a sharpe iudgement shall be to them that be in high places.

text.DRB: Horribly and quickly will he appear to you: because most severe judgement shall be done on them, that bear rule.
text.KJV: 6:6 For mercy will soone pardon the meanest: but mighty men shall be mightily tormented.

text.DRB: For to the little one mercy is granted: but the mighty shall mightily suffer torments.
text.KJV: 6:7 For he which is Lord ouer all, shall feare no mans person: neither shall he stand in awe of any mans greatnesse: for he hath made the small and great, and careth for all alike.

text.DRB: For God will not except any man's person, neither will fear the greatness of any man: because he made the little and the great, & he hath equally care of all.
text.KJV: 6:8 But a sore triall shall come vpon the mighty.

text.DRB: But to the stronger more strong torment is imminent.
text.KJV: 6:9 Unto you therefore, O kings, doe I speake, that yee may learne wisedome, and not fall away.

text.DRB: To you therefore o kings are these my words, that you may learn wisdom, and not fall.
text.KJV: 6:10 For they that keepe holinesse holily, shall be iudged holy: and they that haue learned such things, shall find what to answere.

text.DRB: For they that have kept just things justly, shall be justified: and they that have learned these things, shall find what they may answer.
text.KJV: 6:11 Wherefore set your affection vpon my words, desire them, and yee shall be instructed.
6:12 Wisedome is glorious and neuer fadeth away: yea she is easily seene of them that loue her, and found of such as seeke her.
text.NABRE: 6:12 Resplendent and unfading is Wisdom, and she is readily perceived by those who love her, and found by those who seek her.
text.NRSV: Wisdom is radiant and unfading, and she is easily discerned by those who love her, and is found by those who seek her.
text.DRB: Covet ye therefore my words, and love them, and you shall have discipline.
Wisdom is clear, and such as never fadeth, and is easily seen of them that love her, and is found of them that seek her.
text.KJV: 6:13 She preuenteth them that desire her, in making herselfe first knowen vnto them.

text.DRB: She preventeth them that covet her, that she first may shew herself unto them.
text.KJV: 6:14 Whoso seeketh her earely, shall haue no great trauaile: for he shall find her sitting at his doores.

text.DRB: He that awaketh early to her, shall not labour: for he shall find her sitting at her doors.
text.KJV: 6:15 To thinke therefore vpon her is perfection of wisedome: and who so watcheth for her, shall quickly be without care.

text.DRB: To think therefore of her, is perfect understanding: and he that watcheth for her, shall quickly be secure.
text.KJV: 6:16 For she goeth about seeking such as are worthy of her, sheweth herselfe fauourably vnto them in the wayes, and meeteth them in euery thought.
6:17 For the very true beginning of her, is the desire of discipline, and the care of discipline is loue:
text.NABRE: 6:17 For the first step toward Wisdom is an earnest desire for discipline;
text.NRSV: The beginning of wisdom is the most sincere desire for instruction, and concern for instruction is love of her,
text.DRB: Because she goeth about seeking them that be worthy of her, and in the ways she will shew herself to them cheerfully, and in all providence she will meet them.

text.KJV: 6:18 And loue is the keeping of her lawes; and the giuing heed vnto her lawes, is the assurance of incorruption.
text.NABRE: 6:18 then, care for discipline is love of her; love means the keeping of her laws; To observe her laws is the basis for incorruptibility;
text.NRSV: and love of her is the keeping of her laws, and giving heed to her laws is assurance of incorruption,
text.DRB: For the beginning of her is the most true desire of discipline.

text.KJV: 6:19 And incorruption maketh vs neere vnto God.
text.NABRE: 6:19 and incorruptibility makes one close to God;
text.NRSV: and incorruption brings one near to God;
text.DRB: The care therefore of discipline, is love: and love is keeping of her laws: and the keeping of the laws, is the consummation of incorruption:

text.KJV: 6:20 Therefore the desire of wisedome bringeth to a kingdome.
text.NABRE: 6:20 thus the desire for Wisdom leads to a kingdom.
text.NRSV: so the desire for wisdom leads to a kingdom.
text.DRB: and incorruption maketh to be next to God.
Therefore the desire of wisdom leadeth to the everlasting Kingdom.
text.KJV: 6:21 If your delight be then in thrones and scepters, O ye kings of the people, honour wisedome that yee may raigne for euermore.

text.DRB: If therefore you be delighted with thrones, and with sceptres, o ye kings of the people, love wisdom, that you may reign for ever.
text.KJV: 6:22 As for wisedome what she is, and how she came vp, I will tell you, and will not hide mysteries from you: but will seeke her out from the beginning of her natiuity, & bring the knowledge of her into light, and will not passe ouer the trueth.

text.DRB: Love the light of wisdom all ye that bear rule over peoples.
text.KJV: 6:23 Neither will I goe with consuming enuy: for such a man shall haue no fellowship with wisedome.
6:24 But the multitude of the wise is the welfare of the world: and a wise king is the vpholding of the people.
text.NABRE: 6:24 A multitude of the wise is the safety of the world, and a prudent king, the stability of the people;
text.NRSV: The multitude of the wise is the salvation of the world, and a sensible king is the stability of any people.
text.DRB: But what wisdom is, and how she was made, I will declare: and I will not hide from you the mysteries of God, but from the beginning of her nativity I will search out, and set the knowledge of her into light, and will not let pass the truth:
neither will I go with pining envy: because such a man shall not be partaker of wisdom.
text.KJV: 6:25 Receiue therefore instruction thorough my words, and it shall doe you good.

text.DRB: But the multitude of the wise is the health of the round world: and a wise king is the stability of the people.
Therefore take ye discipline by my words, and it shall profit you.

text.KJV: 7:1 I my selfe also am a mortall man, like to all, and the ofspring of him that was first made of the earth,
text.NABRE: 7:1 Chapter 7 Solomon Is Like All Others I too am a mortal, the same as all the rest, and a descendant of the first one formed of earth. And in my mother’s womb I was molded into flesh
text.NRSV: I also am mortal, like everyone else, a descendant of the first-formed child of earth; and in the womb of a mother I was molded into flesh,
text.DRB: I also certes am a mortal man, like to all, and of the earthly kindred of him that was made first, and in the womb of my mother was I fashioned flesh,
the time of ten months was I brought together in blood, of the seed of man, and the delectation of sleep concurring.
text.KJV: 7:2 And in my mothers wombe was fashioned to be flesh in the time of tenne moneths being compacted in blood, of the seed of man, and the pleasure that came with sleepe.

text.DRB: And I being born received the common air, and fell upon the earth that is made alike, and the first voice like to all men did I put forth weeping.
text.KJV: 7:3 And when I was borne, I drew in the common aire, and fell vpon the earth which is of like nature, and the first voice which I vttered, was crying as all others doe.

text.DRB: I was nourished in swaddling cloths, and great cares.
text.KJV: 7:4 I was nursed in swadling clothes, and that with cares.

text.DRB: For none of the kings had other beginning of nativity.
text.KJV: 7:5 For there is no king that had any other beginning of birth.

text.DRB: There is one entrance therefore into life to all men, and like departure.
text.KJV: 7:6 For all men haue one entrance vnto life, and the like going out.
7:7 Wherefore I prayed, and vnderstanding was giuen mee: I called vpon God, and the spirit of wisedome came to me.
text.NABRE: 7:7 Solomon Prayed and Wisdom and Riches Came to Him Therefore I prayed, and prudence was given me; I pleaded and the spirit of Wisdom came to me.
text.NRSV: Therefore I prayed, and understanding was given me; I called on God, and the spirit of wisdom came to me.
text.DRB: For this cause I wished, and understanding was given me: and I invocated, and the spirit of wisdom came upon me:

text.KJV: 7:8 I preferred her before scepters, and thrones, and esteemed riches nothing in comparison of her.
text.NABRE: 7:8 I preferred her to scepter and throne, And deemed riches nothing in comparison with her,
text.NRSV: I preferred her to scepters and thrones, and I accounted wealth as nothing in comparison with her.
text.DRB: and I preferred her before Kingdoms and thrones, and riches I counted to be nothing in comparison of her.
Neither did I compare the precious stone to her: because all gold in comparison of her, is a little sand, and silver in the sight of her shall be esteemed as clay.
text.KJV: 7:9 Neither compared I vnto her any precious stone, because all gold in respect of her is as a little sand, and siluer shalbe counted as clay before her.

text.DRB: Above health and beauty did I love her, and purposed to have her for light: because her light cannot be extinguished.
text.KJV: 7:10 I loued her aboue health and beautie, and chose to haue her in stead of light: for the light that commeth from her neuer goeth out.

text.DRB: And all good things came to me together with her, and very much honesty by her hands,
text.KJV: 7:11 All good things together came to me with her, and innumerable riches in her hands.

text.DRB: and I rejoiced in all: because this wisdom went before me, and I was ignorant that she is the mother of all these.
text.KJV: 7:12 And I reioyced in them all, because wisedome goeth before them: and I knew not that shee was the mother of them.

text.DRB: Which I learned without fiction, and do communicate without envy, and her honesty I hid not.
text.KJV: 7:13 I learned diligently, and doe communicate her liberally: I doe not hide her riches.

text.DRB: For she is an infinite treasure to men: which who so have, are made partakers of the friendship of God commended for the gifts of discipline.
text.KJV: 7:14 For shee is a treasure vnto men that neuer faileth: which they that vse, become the friends of God: being commended for the gifts that come from learning.

text.DRB: And to me God hath given to speak according to my mind, and to presume things worthy of those, that are given me: because he is the guide of wisdom, and the Creator of the wise:
text.KJV: 7:15 God hath granted me to speake as I would, and to conceiue as is meet for the things that are giuen mee: because it is hee that leadeth vnto wisedome, and directeth the wise.

text.DRB: For in his hand are both we, & our words, and wisdom, and the knowledge & discipline of works.
text.KJV: 7:16 For in his hand are both we and our wordes: all wisedome also and knowledge of workemanship.

text.DRB: For he gave me the true knowledge of those things which are: that I may know the disposition of the round world, and the virtues of the elements,
text.KJV: 7:17 For hee hath giuen mee certaine knowledge of the things that are, namely to know how the world was made, & the operation of the elements:

text.DRB: the beginning, & end, & midst of times, the permutations of changeable seasons, and consummations of times,
text.KJV: 7:18 The beginning, ending, and midst of the times: the alterations of the turning of the Sunne, and the change of seasons:

text.DRB: the courses of the year, and dispositions of the stars,
text.KJV: 7:19 The circuits of yeres, and the positions of starres:

text.DRB: the natures of beasts, and furies of wild beasts, the force of winds, and the cogitations of men, the differences of plants, and virtues of roots,
text.KJV: 7:20 The natures of liuing creatures, and the furies of wilde beasts: the violence of windes, and the reasonings of men: the diuersities of plants, and the vertues of rootes:

text.DRB: & whatsoever are hid things and not foreseen, I have learned: for wisdom the worker of all taught me.
text.KJV: 7:21 And all such things as are either secret or manifest: them I know.

7:22 For wisedome which is the worker of all things, taught mee: for in her is an vnderstanding spirit holy, one onely, manifold, subtile, liuely, cleare, vndefiled, plaine, not subiect to hurt, louing the thing that is good, quicke, which cānot be letted, ready to do good:
text.NABRE: 7:22 for Wisdom, the artisan of all, taught me. Nature and Incomparable Dignity of Wisdom For in her is a spirit intelligent, holy, unique, Manifold, subtle, agile, clear, unstained, certain, Never harmful, loving the good, keen,
text.NRSV: For wisdom, the fashioner of all things, taught me. There is in her a spirit that is intelligent, holy, unique, manifold, subtle, mobile, clear, unpolluted, distinct, invulnerable, loving the good, keen, irresistible,
text.DRB: For in her is the spirit of understanding, holy, only, manifold, subtile, eloquent, moveable, undefiled, sure, sweet, loving, good, sharp, who nothing hindereth well-doing,
gentle, benign, stable, certain, secure, having all power, foreseeing all things, and that containeth all spirits: intelligible, clean, subtile.
text.KJV: 7:23 Kinde to man, stedfast, sure, free from care, hauing all power, ouerseeing all things, and going through all vnderstanding, pure, and most subtile spirits.
7:24 For wisedome is more moouing then any motion: she passeth and goeth through all things by reason of her purenesse.
text.NABRE: 7:24 For Wisdom is mobile beyond all motion, and she penetrates and pervades all things by reason of her purity.
text.NRSV: For wisdom is more mobile than any motion; because of her pureness she pervades and penetrates all things.
text.DRB: For wisdom is more moveable than all moveable things: and reacheth every-where because of her cleanness.

text.KJV: 7:25 For she is the breath of the power of God, and a pure influence flowing from the glory of the Almighty: therefore can no vndefiled thing fall into her.
text.NABRE: 7:25 For she is a breath of the might of God and a pure emanation of the glory of the Almighty; therefore nothing defiled can enter into her.
text.NRSV: For she is a breath of the power of God, and a pure emanation of the glory of the Almighty; therefore nothing defiled gains entrance into her.
text.DRB: For she is a vapour of the power of God, & a certain sincere emanation of the glory of God omnipotent: and therefore no defiled thing cometh unto her.

text.KJV: 7:26 For shee is the brightnesse of the euerlasting light: the vnspotted mirrour of the power of God, and the Image of his goodnesse.
text.NABRE: 7:26 For she is the reflection of eternal light, the spotless mirror of the power of God, the image of his goodness.
text.NRSV: For she is a reflection of eternal light, a spotless mirror of the working of God, and an image of his goodness.
text.DRB: For she is the brightness of eternal light, & the unspotted glass of God's majesty, and the image of his goodness.

text.KJV: 7:27 And being but one she can doe all things: and remayning in her selfe, she maketh all things new: and in all ages entring into holy soules, she maketh them friends of God, & Prophets.
text.NABRE: 7:27 Although she is one, she can do all things, and she renews everything while herself perduring; Passing into holy souls from age to age, she produces friends of God and prophets.
text.NRSV: Although she is but one, she can do all things, and while remaining in herself, she renews all things; in every generation she passes into holy souls and makes them friends of God, and prophets;
text.DRB: And whereas she is one, she can do all things: and permanent in herself she reneweth all things, and by nations transporteth herself into holy souls, she maketh the friends of God, and Prophets.

text.KJV: 7:28 For God loueth none but him, that dwelleth with wisedome.
text.NABRE: 7:28 For God loves nothing so much as the one who dwells with Wisdom.
text.NRSV: for God loves nothing so much as the person who lives with wisdom.
text.DRB: For God loveth none, but him that dwelleth with wisdom.

text.KJV: 7:29 For she is more beautiful then the Sunne, and aboue all the order of starres, being compared with the light, she is found before it.
text.NABRE: 7:29 For she is fairer than the sun and surpasses every constellation of the stars. Compared to light, she is found more radiant;
text.NRSV: She is more beautiful than the sun, and excels every constellation of the stars. Compared with the light she is found to be superior,
text.DRB: For she is more beautiful than the sun, and above all disposition of the stars, being compared to light she is found the first.

text.KJV: 7:30 For after this commeth night: but vice shall not preuaile against wisdome.
text.NABRE: 7:30 though night supplants light, wickedness does not prevail over Wisdom.
text.NRSV: for it is succeeded by the night, but against wisdom evil does not prevail.
text.DRB: For night succeedeth to it, but malice overcometh not wisdom.

text.KJV: 8:1 Wisdome reacheth from one ende to another mightily: and sweetly doeth she order all things.
text.NABRE: 8:1 Chapter 8 Indeed, she spans the world from end to end mightily and governs all things well.
text.NRSV: She reaches mightily from one end of the earth to the other, and she orders all things well.
text.DRB: She reacheth therefore from end unto end mightily, and disposeth all things sweetly.

text.KJV: 8:2 I loued her and sought her out, from my youth I dedesired to make her my spouse, and I was a louer of her beautie.
text.NABRE: 8:2 Wisdom, the Source of Blessings Her I loved and sought after from my youth; I sought to take her for my bride and was enamored of her beauty.
text.NRSV: I loved her and sought her from my youth; I desired to take her for my bride, and became enamored of her beauty.
text.DRB: Her have I loved, and have sought her out from my youth, and have sought to take her for my spouse, and I was made a lover of her beauty.
She glorifieth her nobility, having consociation with God: yea and the Lord of all hath loved her.
text.KJV: 8:3 In that she is conuersant with God, she magnifieth her nobilitie: yea, the Lord of all things himselfe loued her.

text.DRB: For she is the mistress of the discipline of God, & the chooser of his works.
text.KJV: 8:4 For she is priuy to the mysteries of the knowledge of God, and a louer of his workes.

text.DRB: And if riches be desired in life, what is richer than wisdom, which worketh all things?
text.KJV: 8:5 If riches be a possession to be desired in this life: what is richer then wisedome that worketh all things?

text.DRB: And if understanding do work: who is the worker of those things that are, more than she?
text.KJV: 8:6 And if prudence worke; who of all that are, is a more cunning workeman then she?

8:7 And if a man loue righteousnesse, her labours are vertues: for she teacheth temperance and prudence: iustice and fortitude, which are such things as men can haue nothing more profitable in their life.
text.NABRE: 8:7 Or if one loves righteousness, whose works are virtues, She teaches moderation and prudence, righteousness and fortitude, and nothing in life is more useful than these.
text.NRSV: And if anyone loves righteousness, her labors are virtues; for she teaches self-control and prudence, justice and courage; nothing in life is more profitable for mortals than these.
text.DRB: And if a man love justice: her labours have great virtues: for she teacheth sobriety, and prudence, and justice, and strength, than the which nothing is more profitable in life to men.

And if a man desire multitude of knowledge, she knoweth things past, & conjectureth of things to come: she knoweth the subtilties of words, and the solutions of arguments: she knoweth signs and wonders before they be done, and the events of times and ages.
text.KJV: 8:8 If a man desire much experience: she knoweth things of old, and coniectureth aright what is to come: shee knoweth the subtilties of speaches, and can expound darke sentences: she foreseeth signes and wonders, and the euents of seasons and times.

text.DRB: I purposed therefore to bring her to me to live together: knowing that she will communicate unto me of good things, and will be a comfort of my cogitation & tediousness.
text.KJV: 8:9 Therefore I purposed to take her to me to liue with mee, knowing that shee would be a counsellour of good things, and a comfort in cares & griefe.

text.DRB: Ishall have for her sake glory with the multitudes and honour with the ancient being young:
text.KJV: 8:10 For her sake I shall haue estimation among the multitude, and honour with the Elders, though I be yong.

text.DRB: and I shall be found sharp in judgement, and in the sight of the mighty I shall be marvelous, and the faces of Princes will marvel at me.
text.KJV: 8:11 I shall be found of a quicke conceit in iudgement, and shall be admired in the sight of great men.

text.DRB: Holding my peace they shall expect me, and whilst I speak many words, they shall lay their hands on their mouth.
text.KJV: 8:12 When I hold my tongue they shal bide my leisure, and when I speake they shall giue good eare vnto me: if I talke much, they shall lay their handes vpon their mouth.

text.DRB: Moreover by her I shall have immortality: and I shall leave an eternal memory to them, that shall be after me.
text.KJV: 8:13 Moreouer, by the meanes of her, I shall obtaine immortalitie, and leaue behind me an euerlasting memoriall to them that come after me.

text.DRB: Ishall dispose peoples, and nations shall be subject to me.
text.KJV: 8:14 I shall set the people in order, and the nations shalbe subiect vnto me.

text.DRB: Horrible kings hearing shall fear me: in the multitude I shall seem good, and in battle strong.
text.KJV: 8:15 Horrible tyrants shall be afraide when they doe but heare of me, I shall be found good among the multitude, and valiant in warre.

text.DRB: Entering into my house, I shall rest with her: for her conversation hath no bitterness, nor her company tediousness, but joy and gladness.
text.KJV: 8:16 After I am come into mine house, I will repose my selfe with her: for her conuersation hath no bitternes, and to liue with her, hath no sorrow, but mirth and ioy.

text.DRB: Thinking these things with myself, and recording in my heart, that immortality is in the kindred of wisdom,
text.KJV: 8:17 Now when I considered these things in my selfe, and pondered them in mine heart, how that to be allyed vnto wisedome, is immortalitie,

text.DRB: and good delectation in her friendship, and in the works of her hands honesty without defect, and wisdom in the disputation of her talk, and glory in the communication of her words: I went about seeking, that I might take her to me.
text.KJV: 8:18 And great pleasure it is to haue her friendship, and in the workes of her hands are infinite riches, and in the exercise of conference with her, prudence: and in talking with her a good report: I went about seeking how to take her to me.

text.DRB: And I was a witty child, and had gotten a good soul.
text.KJV: 8:19 For I was a wittie child, and had a good spirit.

text.DRB: And whereas I was more good, I came to a body undefiled.
text.KJV: 8:20 Yea rather being good, I came into a body vndefiled.

text.DRB: And as I knew that I could not otherwise be continent, unless God gave it, this very thing also was wisdom, to know whose this gift was: I went to our Lord, and besought him, and said from my whole heart:
text.KJV: 8:21 Neuerthelesse when I perceiued that I could not otherwise obtaine her, except God gaue her me (and that was a point of wisdome also to know whose gift she was) I prayed vnto the Lord, and besought him, and with my whole heart I said:
9:1 O God of my fathers, and Lord of mercy, who hast made all things with thy word,
text.NABRE: 9:1 Chapter 9 Solomon’s Prayer God of my ancestors, Lord of mercy, you who have made all things by your word
text.NRSV: “O God of my ancestors and Lord of mercy, who have made all things by your word,”
text.DRB: God of my fathers, and Lord of mercy, which madest all things with thy Word,

text.KJV: 9:2 And ordained man through thy wisedome, that he should haue dominion ouer the creatures, which thou hast made,
text.NABRE: 9:2 And in your wisdom have established humankind to rule the creatures produced by you,
text.NRSV: “and by your wisdom have formed humankind to have dominion over the creatures you have made,”
text.DRB: and by thy Wisdom didst appoint man, that he should have dominion of the creature, that was made by thee,
that he should dispose the round world in equity and justice, and execute judgement in direction of heart:
text.KJV: 9:3 And order the world according to equitie and righteousnesse, and execute iudgement with an vpright heart:
9:4 Giue me wisedome that sitteth by thy Throne, and reiect me not from among thy children:
text.NABRE: 9:4 Give me Wisdom, the consort at your throne, and do not reject me from among your children;
text.NRSV: “give me the wisdom that sits by your throne, and do not reject me from among your servants.”
text.DRB: give me wisdom the assistant of thy seats, and repel me not from thy children:
because I am thy servant, and the son of thy handmaid, a weak man and of small time, and less to the understanding of judgement and laws.
text.KJV: 9:5 For I thy seruant and sonne of thine handmaide, am a feeble person, and of a short time, and too young for the vnderstanding of iudgement and lawes.

text.DRB: And if one be perfect among the children of men, and thy wisdom be absent from him, he shall be counted for nothing.
text.KJV: 9:6 For though a man be neuer so perfect among the children of men, yet if thy wisedome be not with him, hee shall be nothing regarded.

text.DRB: Thou hast chosen me king to thy people, and judge of thy sons and daughters:
text.KJV: 9:7 Thou hast chosen me to be a king of thy people, and a Iudge of thy sons and daughters:

text.DRB: and badest me build a temple in thy holy mount, and an altar in the city of thy habitation, a similitude of thy holy tabernacle, which thou didst prepare from the beginning:
text.KJV: 9:8 Thou hast commaunded me to build a Temple vpon thy holy mount, and an Altar in the city wherein thou dwellest, a resemblance of the holy Tabernacle which thou hast prepared from the beginning:
9:9 And wisedome was with thee: which knoweth thy workes, and was present when thou madest the world, and knew what was acceptable in thy sight, and right in thy Commaundements.
text.NABRE: 9:9 Now with you is Wisdom, who knows your works and was present when you made the world; Who understands what is pleasing in your eyes and what is conformable with your commands.
text.NRSV: “With you is wisdom, she who knows your works and was present when you made the world; she understands what is pleasing in your sight and what is right according to your commandments.”
text.DRB: and thy wisdom with thee, which knew thy works, which then also was present when thou madest the round world, and knew what was pleasing to thine eyes, and what was direct in thy precepts.

text.KJV: 9:10 O send her out of thy holy heauens, and from the Throne of thy glory, that being present shee may labour with mee, that I may know what is pleasing vnto thee.
text.NABRE: 9:10 Send her forth from your holy heavens and from your glorious throne dispatch her That she may be with me and work with me, that I may know what is pleasing to you.
text.NRSV: “Send her forth from the holy heavens, and from the throne of your glory send her, that she may labor at my side, and that I may learn what is pleasing to you.”
text.DRB: Send her from thy holy heavens, and from the seat of thy greatness, that she may be with me, and may labour with me, that I may know what is acceptable with thee:
for she knoweth all things & understandeth, and shall conduct me in my works soberly, & shall keep me with her might.
text.KJV: 9:11 For she knoweth and vnderstandeth all things, and shee shall leade me soberly in my doings, and preserue me in her power.

text.DRB: And my works shall be acceptable, and I shall govern thy people justly, and shall be worthy of the seats of my father.
text.KJV: 9:12 So shall my workes be acceptable, and then shall I iudge thy people righteously, and be worthy to sit in my fathers seate.
9:13 For what man is hee that can know the counsell of God? or who can thinke what the will of the Lord is?
text.NABRE: 9:13 For who knows God’s counsel, or who can conceive what the Lord intends?
text.NRSV: “For who can learn the counsel of God? Or who can discern what the Lord wills?”
text.DRB: For who of men is able to know the counsel of God? Or who can think what God will?
For the cogitations of mortal men be fearful, and our providences uncertain.
text.KJV: 9:14 For the thoughts of mortall men are miserable, and our deuices are but vncertaine.
9:15 For the corruptible body presseth downe the soule, and the earthy tabernacle weigheth downe the minde that museth vpon many things.
text.NABRE: 9:15 For the corruptible body burdens the soul and the earthly tent weighs down the mind with its many concerns.
text.NRSV: “for a perishable body weighs down the soul, and this earthy tent burdens the thoughtful mind.”
text.DRB: For the body that is corrupted burdeneth the soul, and the earthly habitation presseth down the understanding that thinketh many things.
And we do hardly conjecture the things that are in the earth: and the things that are in sight, we find with labour. But the things that are in the heavens who shall search out?
text.KJV: 9:16 And hardly doe we gesse aright at things that are vpon earth, and with labour doe wee find the things that are before vs: but the things that are in heauen, who hath searched out?

text.DRB: And thy sense who shall know, unless thou give wisdom, and send thy holy Spirit from on high:
text.KJV: 9:17 And thy counsell who hath knowen, except thou giue wisedome, and send thy holy spirit from aboue?

text.DRB: and so the paths of them that are on the earth, may be corrected, and men learn the things that please thee?
text.KJV: 9:18 For so the wayes of them which liued on the earth were reformed, and men were taught the things that are pleasing vnto thee, and were saued through wisedome.

text.DRB: For by wisdom they were healed, whosoever have pleased thee o Lord from the beginning.

text.KJV: 10:1 She preserued the first formed father of the world that was created alone, and brought him out of his fall,
text.NABRE: 10:1 Chapter 10 Wisdom Preserves Her Followers She preserved the first-formed father of the world when he alone had been created; And she raised him up from his fall,
text.NRSV: Wisdom protected the first-formed father of the world, when he alone had been created; she delivered him from his transgression,
text.DRB: She kept him, that was first made of God father of the world, when he was created alone,

text.KJV: 10:2 And gaue him power to rule all things.
text.NABRE: 10:2 and gave him power to rule all things.
text.NRSV: and gave him strength to rule all things.
text.DRB: and she brought him out of his sin, and gave him power to contain all things.
After the unjust departed in his anger from her, by the fury of brother's manslaughter he perished.
text.KJV: 10:3 But when the vnrighteous went away from her in his anger, he perished also in the fury wherwith he murdered his brother.
10:4 For whose cause the earth being drowned with the flood, Wisedome againe preserued it, & directed the course of the righteous, in a piece of wood, of small value.
text.NABRE: 10:4 When on his account the earth was flooded, Wisdom again saved it, piloting the righteous man on frailest wood.
text.NRSV: When the earth was flooded because of him, wisdom again saved it, steering the righteous man by a paltry piece of wood.
text.DRB: For whose cause, when water destroyed the earth, wisdom healed it again, governing the just by contemptible wood.

text.KJV: 10:5 Moreouer, the nations in their wicked conspiracie being confounded, she found out the righteous, and preserued him blamelesse vnto God, and kept him strong against his tender compassion towards his sonne.
text.NABRE: 10:5 She, when the nations were sunk in universal wickedness, knew the righteous man, kept him blameless before God, and preserved him resolute against pity for his child.
text.NRSV: Wisdom also, when the nations in wicked agreement had been confounded, recognized the righteous man and kept him blameless before God, and held him strong in the face of his compassion for his child.
text.DRB: She even in the consent of wickedness, when the nations had confederated themselves, knew the just, and preserved him without blame to God, and in his son's mercy kept the strong.

text.KJV: 10:6 When the vngodly perished, shee deliuered the righteous man, who fled from the fire which fell downe vpon the fiue cities.
text.NABRE: 10:6 She rescued a righteous man from among the wicked who were being destroyed, when he fled as fire descended upon the Pentapolis—
text.NRSV: Wisdom rescued a righteous man when the ungodly were perishing; he escaped the fire that descended on the Five Cities.
text.DRB: She delivered the just, flying from the impious that perished, when the fire came down upon Pentapolis:
to whom for a witness of their wickedness the desert land standeth smoking, and trees having fruits at uncertain season; and the memory of an incredulous soul a standing pillar of salt.
text.KJV: 10:7 Of whose wickednesse euen to this day the waste land that smoketh, is a testimonie, and plants bearing fruite that neuer come to ripenesse: and a standing pillar of salt is a monument of an vnbeleeuing soule.

text.DRB: For pretermitting wisdom they did not only slip in this, that they were ignorant of good things, but they left also unto men a memory of their foolishness, that in those things, in which they sinned in, they could not be hid neither.
text.KJV: 10:8 For regarding not wisedome, they gate not only this hurt, that they knew not the things which were good: but also left behind them to the world a memoriall of their foolishnes: so that in the things wherein they offended, they could not so much as be hid.

text.DRB: But wisdom hath delivered them that observe her from sorrows.
text.KJV: 10:9 But Wisedome deliuered from paine those that attended vpon her.

10:10 When the righteous fled from his brothers wrath, she guided him in right paths: shewed him the kingdome of God: and gaue him knowledge of holy things, made him rich in his trauailes, and multiplied the fruit of his labours.
text.NABRE: 10:10 She, when a righteous man fled from his brother’s anger, guided him in right ways, Showed him the kingdom of God and gave him knowledge of holy things; She prospered him in his labors and made abundant the fruit of his works,
text.NRSV: When a righteous man fled from his brother’s wrath, she guided him on straight paths; she showed him the kingdom of God, and gave him knowledge of holy things.
text.DRB: And the just flying his brother's wrath, she conducted by the right ways, and shewed him the kingdom of God, and gave him the knowledge of the holy, did honest him in labours, and accomplished his labours.
In the fraud of the circumventers of him she was present with him, and made him honourable.
text.KJV: 10:11 In the couetousnesse of such as oppressed him, she stood by him, and made him rich.

text.DRB: She kept him from the enemies, and from seducers she defended him, and gave him a strong fight, that he might overcome, and know that wisdom is mightier than all.
text.KJV: 10:12 She defended him from his enemies, and kept him safe from those that lay in wait, and in a sore conflict she gaue him the victory, that he might knowe that godlinesse is stronger then all.

text.DRB: She forsook not the just being sold, but delivered him from sinners: and she went down with him into the pit,
text.KJV: 10:13 When the righteous was solde, she forsooke him not, but deliuered him from sinne: she went downe with him into the pit,

text.DRB: and in bands left him not, till she brought him the sceptre of a Kingdom, and might against them that oppressed him: and shewed them to be liars, that spotted him, and gave him eternal glory.
text.KJV: 10:14 And left him not in bonds till she brought him the scepter of the kingdom and power against those that oppressed him: as for them that had accused him, she shewed them to be liers, and gaue them perpetuall glory.

text.DRB: The just people, and seed without blame she delivered from the nations, that oppressed them.
text.KJV: 10:15 She deliuered the righteous people, and blamelesse seed from the nation that oppressed them.

text.DRB: She entered into the soul of the servant of God, and stood against dreadful kings in wonders and signs.
text.KJV: 10:16 She entred into the soule of the seruant of the Lord, and withstood dreadfull kings in wonders and signes,

text.DRB: And she rendered to the just the hope of their labours, and conducted them in a marvelous way: and she was unto them for a cover in the day, and for the light of stars by night:
text.KJV: 10:17 Rendred to the righteous a reward of their labours, guided them in a marueilous way, and was vnto them for a couer by day, and a light of starres in the night season:

text.DRB: and she transported them through the Red sea, and carried them over through a great water.
text.KJV: 10:18 Brought them through the red sea, and led them thorow much water.

text.DRB: But their enemies she drowned in the sea, and from the depth of hell she brought them out. Therefore the just took the spoils of the impious,
text.KJV: 10:19 But she drowned their enemies, and cast them vp out of the bottome of the deepe.

text.DRB: and they sang thy holy name o Lord, and thy victorious hand they praised together:
text.KJV: 10:20 Therefore the righteous spoiled the vngodly, & praised thy holy Name, O Lord, and magnified with one accord thine hand that fought for them.

text.DRB: because wisdom hath opened the mouth of the dumb, and the tongues of infants she hath made eloquent.
text.KJV: 10:21 For wisedome opened the mouth of the dumbe, and made the tongues of them that cannot speake, eloquent.
11:1 She prospered their works in the hand of the holy Prophet.
text.NABRE: 11:1 Chapter 11 She prospered their affairs through the holy prophet.
text.NRSV: Wisdom prospered their works by the hand of a holy prophet.
text.DRB: She directed their works in the hands of a holy Prophet.

text.KJV: 11:2 They went thorough the wildernesse that was not inhabited, and pitched tents in places where there lay no way.
text.NABRE: 11:2 III. Special Providence of God During the Exodus Introduction They journeyed through the uninhabited desert, and in lonely places they pitched their tents;
